title: dahlia pinnata var. gracilis
intro: Dahlia pinnata var. gracilis, also known as the Bitter daisy or Swamp dahlia, is a plant species that belongs to the dahlia family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Dahlia pinnata var. gracilis include its potential to treat various ailments such as digestive issues, fever, and skin infections, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and cholesterol. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including those mentioned above.
